markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Majordomo Torc](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4927)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 195

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +13; precise vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet

**Communication** speech ([Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) and 1 imparted language)

**Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +13, [Nobility Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +13

**Int** +3, **Wis** +3, **Cha** +3

**Will** +14 

</column>

---        

Forged in platinum, a _majordomo torc_ is engraved with heraldic insignias along with one language’s alphabet, much like a choker of elocution. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks and the ability to understand, speak, and write the torc’s language. Your excellent elocution reduces the DC of the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to perform an auditory action while [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) from 5 to 3. 

Upon being invested, the torc appoints itself your majordomo and, given the chance, takes over coordinating your social calendar, engagements, and wardrobe. A _majordomo torc_ has a prim, fussy disposition, and although it defers to you, it can grow sardonic if you frequently ignore its advice. The torc has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _majordomo torc_ casts [_befitting attire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=867) on you, usually to your specifications. However the torc can also choose the appearance of the [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) for you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Majordomo Torc

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance checks using the Piano
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 196 **Hands** 2 **Bulk** 16 --- **Perception** +22; precise vision 30 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) hearing 30 feet **Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (any languages the possessing spirit knew in life) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +23, [Music Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +23, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +21 **Int** +3, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +5 **Will** +22 --- When a skilled musician dies before reaching their full potential or accomplishing an important goal, their soul might refuse to move on to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39). Rather than becoming [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), the spirit possesses an instrument they played in life, turning it into an intelligent item and a [virtuoso instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737). (Other phantom instruments exist, but most are heavy.) A _phantom piano_ offers a bargain to any [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=32) or other skilled musician who finds it: receive musical aid as you dedicate yourself to reaching the success the spirit felt they were denied in life. The piano refuses to play for those who reject this deal. If you accept, the piano asks only that you practice with it and perform for increasingly refined and august audiences, requiring a successful [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check appropriate to your proficiency level. The piano sets no strict timeline, but at the GM’s discretion, if you go too long without making progress, the piano can cease cooperating with you until it’s satisfied with your dedication once again. While you keep up your end of the bargain, the piano grants you a +2 item bonus to Performance checks made with it. The piano also provides access to composition spells based on your Performance proficiency rank, as if you had the corresponding feat. The spells you can use comprise a cumulative list, but you can cast them only when using the piano. You must spend the actions and Focus Points required to cast these spells. **Trained:** [_inspire competence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=385), [_inspire courage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=386)
**Expert:** [_hymn of healing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1768), [_inspire defense_](/Spells.aspx?ID=387)
**Master:** [_symphony of the unfettered heart_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1777)
**Legendary:** [_soothing ballad_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1776) A _phantom piano_ has the following activations. **Activate** command; **Effect** The piano shrinks down to a figurine of light Bulk compact enough to store in a pouch, or from figurine form back to instrument form. In figurine form, the piano doesn’t function as an instrument or provide magic other than this activation. **Activate** command; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) the piano provides you.
name: Phantom Piano

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Feint
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 204 **Usage** worn --- Blessed by [Nivi Rhombodazzle](/Deities.aspx?ID=354), this striking silver necklace is adorned with sparkling sapphires that draw the eye of any who see it. Over the years, this necklace has appeared in many paintings and illustrations, usually around the neck of a carefree and daring adventurer or a stylish rake gambling vast sums of coin. Finding this relic is always seen as a sign of great fortune to come, but when that luck runs out, the necklace is lost just as quickly. Wearing the relic, you quickly realize how distracting it can be, granting you a +1 item bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks made to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434) and on [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks made to gamble or determine the outcome of a game of chance. --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [whammy](/Relics.aspx?ID=99)
**Major Gifts**: [clean luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=100), [perception filter](/Relics.aspx?ID=52)
**Grand Gift**: [against all odds](/Relics.aspx?ID=102)
name: Fortune's Favor

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Sense Motive
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A famed detective once owned the long, curved _sleuth’s pipe_, taking it with them on investigations, keeping it in hand or mouth while cogitating. The pipe might have passed down through generations. Or, when an owner dies without heirs, it could go up for auction, be given to family friends, or remain at the scene of a crime to be found later. The _sleuth’s pipe_ has sapience that eventually awakens, allowing the relic to act as a helpful assistant in investigations. Even while this sapience sleeps within the pipe, it grants you a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302), and if you’re an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59), any checks you make to [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=2811). --- **Minor Gifts**: [beginner's luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=97), [repository of knowledge](/Relics.aspx?ID=51)
**Major Gifts**: [intelligent relic](/Relics.aspx?ID=109), [steal luck](/Relics.aspx?ID=101)
**Grand Gift**: [mental bastion](/Relics.aspx?ID=54)
name: Sleuth's Pipe

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Recall Knowledge about historical events
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 205 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- The origins of the _watch of lost ages_, a silver pocket watch, are enigmatic. Its construction matches no known culture, and its appearance shifts back and forth from battered and tarnished to shiny and new without cause or apparent reason. The watch exudes a sense of melancholy and loss, and anyone holding it is acutely aware of the impermanence of all things and the march of time, moving ever forward. This relic might show up in a mysterious ruin or spontaneously appear in a spellcaster’s hand after they cast a [time](/Traits.aspx?ID=712) spell. The relic grants you a +1 item bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about historical subjects. --- **Minor Gifts**: [fervor](/Relics.aspx?ID=93), [sands of the hourglass](/Relics.aspx?ID=104)
**Major Gifts**: [fervent spell](/Relics.aspx?ID=95), [temporal pulse](/Relics.aspx?ID=106)
**Grand Gift**: [time reversal](/Relics.aspx?ID=108)
name: Watch of Lost Ages

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Diplomacy checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloak of Social Graces](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4957)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These cloaks are created from hand-dyed leather decorated with gemstones, appealing to the individual tastes of the owner. The beautiful decorations of these cloaks encourage their owner to wear them no matter the season, making it visibly part of their unique style. Wearing the cloak boosts self-confidence, granting a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks. 

**Activate—Overcome Self-Doubt** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You readjust your cloak, straightening the collar and brushing out any wrinkles. You gain the effects of a _[heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555)_ spell for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kinetic Wraps of Strength](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4960)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 50

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

**Access** You're from Hermea

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tightly woven strips strengthen the hands and arms, allowing for the wearer to increase their application of force while lifting heavy loads, and shoving past blockades. Promise sculptors primarily use them to handle large blocks of stone, moving them around as they prepare to carve them. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), and [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380). 

**Activate—Burst of Might** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt an Athletics check to Force Open, Reposition, or Shove;  **Effect** You roll the skill check twice and take the better result.

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Seek or Track creatures that have blood.
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Bloodstride Blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4961)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=710" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 64

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This item is restricted due to the teleportation effect._

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Sawtooth Saber](/Weapons.aspx?ID=423)
</row>

**Access** You're a member of the Red Mantis Assassins

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [cunning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=885) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) sawtooth saber_ appears rusty but never loses its edge. It’s drawn to the blood of your targets. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) creatures that have blood. 

**Activate—Call of the Bloodthirst** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet takes [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) damage;  **Effect** You cast _[translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)_, appearing adjacent to the triggering creature, then [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) it with this weapon. The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bloodstride Blade

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to visual Perception checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Gaze of the Mantis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4963)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 64

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Seeing all Red Mantis contracts is outside the scope of PFS play._

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're a member of the Red Mantis Assassins

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These glasses have faceted red lenses like those of a mantis and are popular as a less ostentatious alternative to a full mask. While wearing the _gaze of the mantis_, your field of view increases to give you better awareness of your surroundings. You gain a +2 item bonus to visual Perception checks and cannot be [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375). 

**Activate—Target Sense** <actions string="Single Action" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re a Red Mantis assassin; **Effect** You learn the distance to and direction of all creatures that have a Red Mantis contract on them within 10 miles. If one of these creatures is within 100 feet, you also know any afflictions, conditions, and spells affecting them. This awareness lasts for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gaze of the Mantis

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Coral Aspect](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 78

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from the Mordant Spire

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of petrified wood, this mask evokes the form of a coral reef. When you wear it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), and any armor you’re wearing gains the [aquadynamic](/Traits.aspx?ID=792) trait. 

**Activate—Cohabitate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 minute, your body becomes a mobile coral reef, letting much smaller creatures pass through or shelter in it. Creatures at least one size category smaller than you can share your space. Allies that share your space gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC, and you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to any damage caused by a creature sharing the same space as you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Coral Aspect

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 78

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Pathfinder Society does not use vehicle rules_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from the Mordant Spire

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The swirls on this wooden mask—often worn by Mordant Censors—move and shift, reflecting the shape of the nearest currents. Wearing the mask grants you a +1 item bonus to [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) and [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks. In addition, it allows you to pilot a boat with simply a thought. When you pilot a vehicle, you can keep both hands free and [Drive](/Actions.aspx?ID=3517) from any location on the vehicle. 

**Activate—Set the Course** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are aboard a vehicle; **Effect** You become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute or until you leave the vehicle, whichever comes first, and can use the extra action to [Take Control](/Actions.aspx?ID=3520) of the vehicle or, if you’re already piloting it, Drive or [Stop](/Actions.aspx?ID=3519) the vehicle.

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Seek, Search, or Sense Motive against things that intend harm to you.
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Whispering Veil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4971)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [High Seas](/Sources.aspx?ID=351) pg. 78

**Price** 2,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You're from the Mordant Spire

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved concentric circles surround the eye and ear holes on this dark wooden mask, which lets you hear the whispers that fill the Mordant Spire. The barely audible susurrus that fills your ears advises you of peril, granting you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks for initiative and to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), [Search](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630), or [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) against things that intend harm to you, at the GM’s discretion, such as hostile creatures and haunts. If you tie a foe’s initiative roll, you go first. 

**Activate—Spire Power** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are about to attempt a check that your mythic calling would allow you to spend a Mythic Point on;  **Effect** You draw on the power of the Mordant Spire. You gain 1 Mythic Point, which you can spend to attempt the check. If you don’t spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost. You do not regain a Mythic Point if you critically succeed on the check, even if it’s the first time you did so that day, but still do so on the subsequent critical success, as usual. 

**Activate—Unforeseen Alert** <actions string="Free Action" /> ;    **Requirements** A creature or hazard you aren’t aware of targets you or includes you in an area effect, and you aren’t [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66); **Effect** The whispers warn you of the impending danger. You immediately gain a reaction that you can use only to respond to the triggering action or effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whispering Veil

**Activate—Raise the Tide** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast _[control water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473)_. If you cast it at night, the spell’s area becomes 100 feet long by 100 feet wide.

[Artisan's Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 287

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You need these tools to create items from raw materials with the [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) skill. Different sets are needed for different work, as determined by the GM; for example, blacksmith's tools differ from woodworker's tools. If you wear your artisan's tools, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them. 

You can use a set of tools with 1 hand if you're [wearing them](/Rules.aspx?ID=1456) or 2 if you're holding them.

You need thieves' tools to [Pick Locks](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) or [Disable Devices](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) (of some types) using the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) skill. If your thieves' tools are broken, you can repair them by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your tools; this doesn't require using the [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) action. If you wear your thieves' tools, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them. 

You can use a set of tools with 1 hand if you're [wearing them](/Rules.aspx?ID=1456) or 2 if you're holding them.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scholarly Journal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=64)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 291

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Scholarly journals are uncommon. Each scholarly journal is a folio on a very specific topic, such as vampires or the history of a single town or neighborhood of a city. If you spend 1 minute referencing an academic journal before attempting a skill check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the subject, you gain a +1 item bonus to the check. A compendium of journals costs five times as much as a single journal and requires both hands to use; each compendium contains several journals and grants its bonus on a broader topic, such as all undead or a whole city. The GM determines what scholarly journals are available in any location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scholarly Journal

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Breastplate of Command](/Equipment.aspx?ID=148)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 557

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 resilient [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_ is made from shining bronze overlaid with reinforcing golden panels in the shape of lion’s heads. Wearing this breastplate grants you a commanding aura. You gain a +2 item bonus to Diplomacy checks, but you take a –2 item penalty to Stealth checks to Hide and Sneak and Deception checks to Impersonate.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grant allies within 100 feet a +2 status bonus to saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects for 1 minute. When you activate this ability, each affected ally who’s [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) reduces their frightened value by 1.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Celestial Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=149)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 557

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of _+2 resilient [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9)_ is made of fine white links of a strange and slightly translucent pale metal, and the sleeves and skirt are fashioned into smaller trails that resemble feathers. Unlike normal chain mail, celestial armor has no Speed reduction, its armor check penalty is 0, and its Bulk is 1. 

 You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against fiends. You appear radiant while you wear the armor, giving you a +2 item bonus to Diplomacy checks against all creatures except fiends.

 If you are not good, you are drained 2 while wearing _celestial armor_. You can’t recover from this condition while wearing the armor.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor sprouts glowing wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet. The wings shed bright light in a 40-foot radius (and dim light to the next 40 feet). The wings fade away after 10 minutes.

**Craft Requirements** You are good.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Performance checks to play the _horn of blasting_
markdown:
**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 573 **Price** 700 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- A _horn of blasting_ is a bright brass trumpet. It can be played as an instrument, granting a +2 item bonus to your [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Blowing into the horn with destructive intent, you create a blast note targeting one creature within 30 feet. The blast deals 3d6 sonic damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You blow even louder to create an intense blast wave in a 30-foot cone that deals 8d6 sonic damage. Each creature attempts a DC 28 Fortitude save with the following effects **Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is deafened for 2d6 rounds.

item_bonus_note: checks to Recall Knowledge
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Possibility Tome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=262)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 574

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An array of semiprecious stones is set into the ornate silver and beaten copper cover of this thick and weighty tome. If you open the book before it’s been activated, its vellum pages are blank and pristine, but once activated, words dance and swim onto the pages before your eyes.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);     **Effect** As you flip through the book, you think about a broad topic you want to know more about. Choose one skill: Arcana, Crafting, Medicine, Nature, Occultism, Religion, Society, or a single subcategory of Lore. The book’s pages fill with information about that skill, though only you can see the information. While the pages are full, you can spend an Interact action perusing the book just before attempting a check to Recall Knowledge with the chosen skill. This grants you a +3 item bonus to the check, and if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead. The information within the book disappears after 24 hours or when the tome is activated again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Possibility Tome

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Possibility Tome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=262)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 574

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An array of semiprecious stones is set into the ornate silver and beaten copper cover of this thick and weighty tome. If you open the book before it’s been activated, its vellum pages are blank and pristine, but once activated, words dance and swim onto the pages before your eyes.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);     **Effect** As you flip through the book, you think about a broad topic you want to know more about. Choose one skill: Arcana, Crafting, Medicine, Nature, Occultism, Religion, Society, or a single subcategory of Lore. The book’s pages fill with information about that skill, though only you can see the information. While the pages are full, you can spend an Interact action perusing the book just before attempting a check to Recall Knowledge with the chosen skill. This grants you a +3 item bonus to the check, and if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead. The information within the book disappears after 24 hours or when the tome is activated again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Possibility Tome

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mattock of the Titans](/Equipment.aspx?ID=391)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 601

**Price** 36,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 16
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greatpick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=23)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This 15-foot-long [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) digging tool is far too big for even a Large creature to wield, though if you’re a Small or larger creature, you can wield it while wearing a [_belt of giant strength_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=398), as though it were appropriately sized for you and had 2 Bulk. The GM might also allow you to wield the mattock if you have some other means of wielding oversized weapons, such as if you’re a Large barbarian with the [giant instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=4) or are a Huge creature. When it’s used as a weapon, the _mattock of the titans_ has the statistics of a _+3 greater striking [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) adamantine [greatpick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=23)_.

 While you’re wielding the _mattock of the titans_, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks. You can use it to loosen or push soft earth at a rate of 1 minute per 5-foot cube or smash through solid stone at the rate of 5 minutes per 5-foot-cube.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You dig furiously with the mattock to non-magically replicate the effects of an [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=95) spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a titan, and the initial raw materials must include 8,100 gp of adamantine.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mattock of the Titans

These gem-studded golden anklets give you a +3 item bonus to Acrobatics checks. When you invest the anklets, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 603 **Price** 15,000 gp **Usage** worn belt **Bulk** L --- This thick leather belt is decorated with a buckle carved from glittering quartz in the shape of a fist. You gain a +3 item bonus to Athletics checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to lift a heavy object, Escape, and Force Open. When you invest the belt, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. **Activate** Interact; **Trigger** You are targeted by a thrown rock attack, or a rock would fall on you; **Effect** Attempt an Athletics check to grab the triggering rock. Use the Athletics DC of the creature throwing the rock, the DC of the hazard or other effect, or DC 35 if no other DC is applicable. You must have a free hand to catch the rock, but you can Release anything you’re holding in a hand as part of this reaction. **Success** You safely catch the rock, take no damage, and are now holding the rock.
**Failure** You take half damage.
**Critical Failure** You take full damage.
name: Belt of Giant Strength

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An elegant, colorful gem cut into a complex geometric pattern is slotted into a narrow metal band that fits around your brow. You gain a +3 item bonus to checks to Recall Knowledge, regardless of the skill. When you invest the diadem, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. This gives you additional trained skills and languages, as normal for increasing your Intelligence score. You must select skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same _diadem of intellect_, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain the effects of [_hypercognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=156).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times. When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again. There are various types of _aeon stones_, each with a different shape, color, and magical effect. Each _aeon stone_ also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470). Aeon Stone (Clear Spindle) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 325 gp --- You don’t need to eat or drink while this _aeon stone_ is invested by you. This _aeon stone_ doesn’t function until it has been worn continuously for a week and invested each day therein. If it’s invested by someone else, this interval starts over. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Dull Gray) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 9 gp --- A _dull gray aeon stone_ has lost its special magical properties, sometimes as a result of overusing a _tourmaline sphere_ or _pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stone_. It still orbits your head like any other _aeon stone_ and can thus serve as a stylish, hands-free option for various spells that target an object, like [_continual flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=50). _Dull gray aeon stones_ have no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Gold Nodule) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 230 gp --- When a _gold nodule aeon stone_ is created, its creator chooses a language they know to store within the crystal. When you invest the stone, you gain the ability to understand, speak, and write that language. The resonant power allows you to cast [_comprehend language_](/Spells.aspx?ID=46) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Lavender and Green Ellipsoid) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 30,000 gp --- This functions as a _pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stone_, but it casts an 8th-level [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell with a counteract modifier of +31. The resonant power allows you to cast [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) and [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) as arcane innate spells at will. Aeon Stone (Orange Prism) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _orange prism aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this _aeon stone_. **Activate** envision; **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell’s level is 1 higher (maximum 10th level) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Pale Lavender Ellipsoid) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 2,200 gp --- This aeon stone must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power allows you to cast the [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) cantrip as an arcane innate spell. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A spell targets you; **Effect** The stone casts a 6th-level [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell in an attempt to counteract the triggering spell, with a counteract modifier of +22. This can be used only on spells that specifically target you—not area spells that don’t have targets. If it succeeds, it counteracts the spell for all targets if other creatures were targeted in addition to you. Each time you activate this _aeon stone_, attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the stone permanently turns into a _dull gray aeon stone_. Aeon Stone (Pink Rhomboid) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 1,900 gp --- When you invest this stone, you gain 15 temporary Hit Points. If the stone’s effects are suppressed, you lose any of the temporary Hit Points remaining until it returns. The temporary Hit Points refresh during your daily preparations; they do not refresh if you re-invest the stone, or invest another _pink rhomboid aeon stone_, before then. The resonant power allows you to cast the [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) cantrip as a divine innate spell. Aeon Stone (Tourmaline Sphere) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this _aeon stone_ automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _dull gray aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones. The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-level [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Black Pearl) **Source** [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 63 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters of 10th level or higher who are from the High Seas have access to black pearl aeon stones_ **Price** 2,000 gp **Usage** worn --- This black pearl sparkles with light. The resonant power allows you to cast [sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) once per day. **Activate** envision; **Trigger** you are targeted by a mental effect; **Effect** You receive a +4 status bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect. Whether or not your save is successful, the aeon stone attempts a counteract check at +30 to immediately reflect a copy of the effect back at the originator, targeting it using the creature’s own relevant statistics but controlling the effect as if you had cast it.
Every time you activate this aeon stone, attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the stone shatters and loses its magical properties forever. Aeon Stone (Agate Ellipsoid) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 130 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This aeon stone allows you to cast 2nd-level [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) as a divine innate spell once per day. The resonant power causes the _augury_ spell from the aeon stone to always succeed at the DC 6 flat check to give an answer other than “nothing.” Aeon Stone (Azure Briolette) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 85 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This _aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. **Activate** envision; **Trigger** An enemy fails its save against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) spell you cast using one of your spell slots; **Effect** You gain temporary Hit Points equal to twice the spell's level until your next turn.

The resonant power grants a separate activation. **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You gain the effect of a [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=35) arcane cantrip. Aeon Stone (Dusty Rose Prism) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 50 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This _aeon stone_ allows you to cast the 1st-level [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) cantrip as an arcane innate spell, surrounding yourself in pink energy. The resonant power increases the damage prevented by your _aeon stone's shield_ spell from 5 to 10. Aeon Stone (Pale Orange Rhomboid) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 40,000 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- The first time each day you die, your vital and spiritual essences mingle together and reside within this _aeon stone_. You have a limited sense of how many life forces are currently within 30 feet of the stone, which you can potentially use to determine when it's safe to call on the stone's power. At any time within the next hour after your body died, you can spend 1 minute concentrating in order to return to your body. As long as your body is still intact, you are no longer dead— you are restored to 1 HP with the [doomed 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and [wounded 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) conditions. You can benefit from the effects of only one _pale orange rhomboid aeon stone_ in a given day. The resonant power allows you to wait more than an hour to return to your body with the _aeon stone_; as long as the _wayfinder_ remains on the body, your body doesn't decay and you can return at any point. Aeon Stone (Pearlescent Pyramid) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 900 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- While invested, this _aeon stone_ grants the benefit of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune to your weapons and unarmed attacks. The resonant power grants a special activation. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** You gain the effects of [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) for 1 round. Aeon Stone (Pearly White Spindle) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 60 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- When you invest this _aeon stone_, it slowly starts healing your wounds, restoring 1 HP every minute. The resonant power grants you resistance 1 to negative damage. Aeon Stone (Western Star) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 122 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 225 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This _aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Effect** You activate the aeon stone to gain the effects of a 1st-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159). The resonant power allows you to render all of your _aeon stones_ and your _wayfinder_ [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) whenever you use the activation to gain the effects of _illusory disguise_. Aeon Stone (Clear Quartz Octagon) **Source** [Pathfinder #179: Cradle of Quartz](/Sources.aspx?ID=122) pg. 79 **Price** 90 gp **Usage** worn --- This clear quartz stone appears to have been smashed and then repaired by pouring molten copper into the cracks and fitting the shards back together using the copper as a sort of mortar. When a non-magical item in your possession with a value of 90 gp or less and a Bulk of 1 Bulk or less would become [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), this _aeon stone_ automatically activates, preventing the item from breaking by restoring the item's current Hit Points to the item's maximum Hit Points. The stone then turns permanently into a _dull gray aeon stone_. The stone's resonant power allows you to cast 1st-level [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Amber Sphere) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 9,800 gp --- If you are [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), your body regains much of the appearance it had in life, and you gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) yourself as a living creature. If you're alive, your appearance is the healthiest version of yourself, and you gain a +2 item bonus to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) that involves your strength or vigor. The resonant power of the _amber sphere_ grants you a special activation to improve your salubrious appearance to a lurid extreme. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The stone casts a 7th-level [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=185) on you (DC 34 Will), causing your appearance to burst with a profusion of shocking vigor: your mouth fills with large and bright teeth, your hair animates in grasping tresses, your face flushes with bright blood that seeps from your skin, or similar. The image is unique to each observer, but you remain recognizably yourself regardless of the illusion's form. Aeon Stone (Black Disc) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 15,000 gp --- This _aeon stone_ slowly infuses negative energy into your body. The stone deals 1 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to you (10 negative damage per minute) so long as it's invested, and you can't recover from persistent negative damage while you have the stone invested. The resonant power grants you resistance 10 to good damage and to positive damage. Aeon Stone (Mottled Ellipsoid) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 6,100 gp --- This _aeon stone_ alters your life force. You gain [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), which means you are damaged by positive energy and not healed by [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) effects. You don't take negative damage, and you are healed by negative effects that heal [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). The resonant power allows you to cast 7th-level [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=108) once per day. Aeon Stone (Rainbow Prism) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 2,200 gp --- While this _aeon stone_ orbits your head, the flat part of its base tumbles to briefly face creatures you can see within 30 feet, as though the stone is watching them. You can activate this stone in two ways. **Activate** envision; **Effect** The _aeon stone_ captures the image of a creature of your size that you can see within 30 feet. It can have up to 3 images captured at a time; if you capture a fourth, you decide which image it replaces. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** three times per day; **Requirements** The prism is storing at least one image of a creature of your size; **Effect** The _aeon stone_ casts a 3rd-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) on you, which must be of one of the creatures it has currently captured. While you are under the effects of this spell, the _aeon stone_ is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26).
The resonant power allows the stone to capture up to 5 images. If removed from the _[wayfinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470)_, you must decide which images to lose. Aeon Stone (Cymophane Cabochon) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The cymophane cabochon aeon stone only provides its item bonus to Perception checks and DCs against Hide, Sneak and Steal actions, not a universal bonus to Perception checks and DCs_ **Price** 650 gp --- Jabalis use the _cymophane cabochon aeon stone_ to keep an eye on their belongings and surroundings. You gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks and DCs against [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), and [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) actions. The resonant power allows you to cast [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Olivine Pendeloque) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98 **Price** 1,200 gp --- An _olivine pendeloque aeon stone_ imparts the calmness of still earth and a solidity of thought, granting you a +3 item bonus to saving throws against effects that cause the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions. **Activate—Still Earth** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You become confused, frightened, or stupefied; **Effect** The stone suppresses the triggering effect for 1 minute, but the calming urges impose a –1 status penalty to your attack rolls. Aeon Stone (Polished Pebble) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The polished pebble aeon stone only grants its item bonus to Fortitude saves and DCs against attempts to grapple or swallow the holder; it does not provide a universal bonus to Fortitude saves._ **Price** 50 gp --- A _polished pebble aeon stone_ imbues you with the strength of the earth, granting a +1 item bonus to saves and DCs against attempts to grapple or swallow you. The stone's resonant power allows you to cast [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) as a primal innate spell once per day. You can target only surfaces, not objects, with this spell.
name: Aeon Stone (Orange Prism)

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times. When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again. There are various types of _aeon stones_, each with a different shape, color, and magical effect. Each _aeon stone_ also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470). This _aeon stone_ doesn’t function until it has been worn continuously for a week and invested each day therein. If it’s invested by someone else, this interval starts over. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. The resonant power of the _amber sphere_ grants you a special activation to improve your salubrious appearance to a lurid extreme. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The stone casts a 7th-level [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=185) on you (DC 34 Will), causing your appearance to burst with a profusion of shocking vigor: your mouth fills with large and bright teeth, your hair animates in grasping tresses, your face flushes with bright blood that seeps from your skin, or similar. The image is unique to each observer, but you remain recognizably yourself regardless of the illusion's form. Aeon Stone (Black Disc) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 15,000 gp --- This _aeon stone_ slowly infuses negative energy into your body. The stone deals 1 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to you (10 negative damage per minute) so long as it's invested, and you can't recover from persistent negative damage while you have the stone invested. The resonant power grants you resistance 10 to good damage and to positive damage. Aeon Stone (Mottled Ellipsoid) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 6,100 gp --- This _aeon stone_ alters your life force. You gain [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), which means you are damaged by positive energy and not healed by [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) effects. You don't take negative damage, and you are healed by negative effects that heal [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). The resonant power allows you to cast 7th-level [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=108) once per day. Aeon Stone (Rainbow Prism) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 2,200 gp --- While this _aeon stone_ orbits your head, the flat part of its base tumbles to briefly face creatures you can see within 30 feet, as though the stone is watching them. You can activate this stone in two ways. **Activate** envision; **Effect** The _aeon stone_ captures the image of a creature of your size that you can see within 30 feet. It can have up to 3 images captured at a time; if you capture a fourth, you decide which image it replaces. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** three times per day; **Requirements** The prism is storing at least one image of a creature of your size; **Effect** The _aeon stone_ casts a 3rd-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) on you, which must be of one of the creatures it has currently captured. While you are under the effects of this spell, the _aeon stone_ is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26).
The resonant power allows the stone to capture up to 5 images. If removed from the _[wayfinder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470)_, you must decide which images to lose. Aeon Stone (Cymophane Cabochon) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The cymophane cabochon aeon stone only provides its item bonus to Perception checks and DCs against Hide, Sneak and Steal actions, not a universal bonus to Perception checks and DCs_ **Price** 650 gp --- Jabalis use the _cymophane cabochon aeon stone_ to keep an eye on their belongings and surroundings. You gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks and DCs against [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63), and [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) actions. The resonant power allows you to cast [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Olivine Pendeloque) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98 **Price** 1,200 gp --- An _olivine pendeloque aeon stone_ imparts the calmness of still earth and a solidity of thought, granting you a +3 item bonus to saving throws against effects that cause the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions. **Activate—Still Earth** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You become confused, frightened, or stupefied; **Effect** The stone suppresses the triggering effect for 1 minute, but the calming urges impose a –1 status penalty to your attack rolls. Aeon Stone (Polished Pebble) **Source** [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 98 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The polished pebble aeon stone only grants its item bonus to Fortitude saves and DCs against attempts to grapple or swallow the holder; it does not provide a universal bonus to Fortitude saves._ **Price** 50 gp --- A _polished pebble aeon stone_ imbues you with the strength of the earth, granting a +1 item bonus to saves and DCs against attempts to grapple or swallow you. The stone's resonant power allows you to cast [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) as a primal innate spell once per day. You can target only surfaces, not objects, with this spell.
name: Aeon Stone (Orange Prism)

markdown:
**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times. When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again. There are various types of _aeon stones_, each with a different shape, color, and magical effect. Each _aeon stone_ also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470). Aeon Stone (Clear Spindle) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 325 gp --- You don’t need to eat or drink while this _aeon stone_ is invested by you. This _aeon stone_ doesn’t function until it has been worn continuously for a week and invested each day therein. If it’s invested by someone else, this interval starts over. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Dull Gray) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 9 gp --- A _dull gray aeon stone_ has lost its special magical properties, sometimes as a result of overusing a _tourmaline sphere_ or _pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stone_. It still orbits your head like any other _aeon stone_ and can thus serve as a stylish, hands-free option for various spells that target an object, like [_continual flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=50). _Dull gray aeon stones_ have no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Gold Nodule) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 230 gp --- When a _gold nodule aeon stone_ is created, its creator chooses a language they know to store within the crystal. When you invest the stone, you gain the ability to understand, speak, and write that language. The resonant power allows you to cast [_comprehend language_](/Spells.aspx?ID=46) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Lavender and Green Ellipsoid) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 30,000 gp --- This functions as a _pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stone_, but it casts an 8th-level [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell with a counteract modifier of +31. The resonant power allows you to cast [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) and [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) as arcane innate spells at will. Aeon Stone (Orange Prism) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _orange prism aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this _aeon stone_. **Activate** envision; **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell’s level is 1 higher (maximum 10th level) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Pale Lavender Ellipsoid) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 2,200 gp --- This aeon stone must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power allows you to cast the [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) cantrip as an arcane innate spell. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A spell targets you; **Effect** The stone casts a 6th-level [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell in an attempt to counteract the triggering spell, with a counteract modifier of +22. This can be used only on spells that specifically target you—not area spells that don’t have targets. If it succeeds, it counteracts the spell for all targets if other creatures were targeted in addition to you. Each time you activate this _aeon stone_, attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the stone permanently turns into a _dull gray aeon stone_. Aeon Stone (Pink Rhomboid) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 1,900 gp --- When you invest this stone, you gain 15 temporary Hit Points. If the stone’s effects are suppressed, you lose any of the temporary Hit Points remaining until it returns. The temporary Hit Points refresh during your daily preparations; they do not refresh if you re-invest the stone, or invest another _pink rhomboid aeon stone_, before then. The resonant power allows you to cast the [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) cantrip as a divine innate spell. Aeon Stone (Tourmaline Sphere) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 604 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this _aeon stone_ automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _dull gray aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones. The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-level [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Black Pearl) **Source** [World Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=6) pg. 63 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters of 10th level or higher who are from the High Seas have access to black pearl aeon stones_ **Price** 2,000 gp **Usage** worn --- This black pearl sparkles with light. The resonant power allows you to cast [sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) once per day. **Activate** envision; **Trigger** you are targeted by a mental effect; **Effect** You receive a +4 status bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect. Whether or not your save is successful, the aeon stone attempts a counteract check at +30 to immediately reflect a copy of the effect back at the originator, targeting it using the creature’s own relevant statistics but controlling the effect as if you had cast it.
Every time you activate this aeon stone, attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the stone shatters and loses its magical properties forever. Aeon Stone (Agate Ellipsoid) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 130 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This aeon stone allows you to cast 2nd-level [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) as a divine innate spell once per day. The resonant power causes the _augury_ spell from the aeon stone to always succeed at the DC 6 flat check to give an answer other than “nothing.” Aeon Stone (Azure Briolette) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 85 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This _aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. **Activate** envision; **Trigger** An enemy fails its save against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) spell you cast using one of your spell slots; **Effect** You gain temporary Hit Points equal to twice the spell's level until your next turn.

The resonant power grants a separate activation. **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You gain the effect of a [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=35) arcane cantrip. Aeon Stone (Dusty Rose Prism) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 50 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This _aeon stone_ allows you to cast the 1st-level [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) cantrip as an arcane innate spell, surrounding yourself in pink energy. The resonant power increases the damage prevented by your _aeon stone's shield_ spell from 5 to 10. Aeon Stone (Pale Orange Rhomboid) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 40,000 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- The first time each day you die, your vital and spiritual essences mingle together and reside within this _aeon stone_. You have a limited sense of how many life forces are currently within 30 feet of the stone, which you can potentially use to determine when it's safe to call on the stone's power. At any time within the next hour after your body died, you can spend 1 minute concentrating in order to return to your body. As long as your body is still intact, you are no longer dead— you are restored to 1 HP with the [doomed 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and [wounded 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) conditions. You can benefit from the effects of only one _pale orange rhomboid aeon stone_ in a given day. The resonant power allows you to wait more than an hour to return to your body with the _aeon stone_; as long as the _wayfinder_ remains on the body, your body doesn't decay and you can return at any point. Aeon Stone (Pearlescent Pyramid) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 900 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- While invested, this _aeon stone_ grants the benefit of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune to your weapons and unarmed attacks. The resonant power grants a special activation. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** You gain the effects of [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) for 1 round. Aeon Stone (Pearly White Spindle) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 121 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 60 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- When you invest this _aeon stone_, it slowly starts healing your wounds, restoring 1 HP every minute. The resonant power grants you resistance 1 to negative damage. Aeon Stone (Western Star) **Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 122 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters affiliated with the Pathfinder Society have access to this item_ **Price** 225 gp **Usage** worn **Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society. --- This _aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Effect** You activate the aeon stone to gain the effects of a 1st-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159). The resonant power allows you to render all of your _aeon stones_ and your _wayfinder_ [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) whenever you use the activation to gain the effects of _illusory disguise_. Aeon Stone (Clear Quartz Octagon) **Source** [Pathfinder #179: Cradle of Quartz](/Sources.aspx?ID=122) pg. 79 **Price** 90 gp **Usage** worn --- This clear quartz stone appears to have been smashed and then repaired by pouring molten copper into the cracks and fitting the shards back together using the copper as a sort of mortar. When a non-magical item in your possession with a value of 90 gp or less and a Bulk of 1 Bulk or less would become [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), this _aeon stone_ automatically activates, preventing the item from breaking by restoring the item's current Hit Points to the item's maximum Hit Points. The stone then turns permanently into a _dull gray aeon stone_. The stone's resonant power allows you to cast 1st-level [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Amber Sphere) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 9,800 gp --- If you are [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), your body regains much of the appearance it had in life, and you gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) yourself as a living creature. If you're alive, your appearance is the healthiest version of yourself, and you gain a +2 item bonus to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) that involves your strength or vigor. The resonant power of the _amber sphere_ grants you a special activation to improve your salubrious appearance to a lurid extreme. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The stone casts a 7th-level [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=185) on you (DC 34 Will), causing your appearance to burst with a profusion of shocking vigor: your mouth fills with large and bright teeth, your hair animates in grasping tresses, your face flushes with bright blood that seeps from your skin, or similar. The image is unique to each observer, but you remain recognizably yourself regardless of the illusion's form. The stone deals 1 [persistent negative damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to you (10 negative damage per minute) so long as it's invested, and you can't recover from persistent negative damage while you have the stone invested. The resonant power grants you resistance 10 to good damage and to positive damage. You don't take negative damage, and you are healed by negative effects that heal [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). The resonant power allows you to cast 7th-level [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=108) once per day. Aeon Stone (Rainbow Prism) **Source** [Pathfinder #185: A Taste of Ashes](/Sources.aspx?ID=188) pg. 77 **Price** 2,200 gp --- While this _aeon stone_ orbits your head, the flat part of its base tumbles to briefly face creatures you can see within 30 feet, as though the stone is watching them.
