_Fashionable wayfinders_ allow you to be dressed for success in almost any situation. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _fashionable wayfinder_ can attune to up to three sets of mundane clothing in your possession during your daily preparations. A _fashionable wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You call one of the attuned sets of clothing onto yourself, changing clothes instantly and leaving you either holding the old set of clothing or with the old set folded neatly on the floor if you don't have a free hand to hold it. This activation can't change you into or out of a suit of armor of any kind. While the activation can transport the chosen attuned set of clothing onto you from up to 60 feet away, it can't transport any other creatures or items that might have been stored in the clothing.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

---

_Homeward wayfinders_ are powerful escape tools in the hands of a Pathfinder agent, drawing upon a special connection to the Maze of the Open Road. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _homeward wayfinder_ attunes itself to the most recent Pathfinder Lodge you've visited and has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You teleport back to the attuned Pathfinder Lodge, as long as you are no more than 100 miles away. This [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effect fails if you would bring another creature with you, even if you're carrying it in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space.

level="2"

Hummingbird Wayfinder
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

_Hummingbird wayfinders_ blend a few Arcadian secrets into the original _wayfinder_ design, allowing the _wayfinder_ to gain mobility for a time and act as a spy. In addition to the effects of a _wayfinder_, a _hummingbird wayfinder_ has the following activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cause the _wayfinder_ to transform into a Tiny bee hummingbird, only 2 inches long. Other than the _wayfinder_ being a visible bird, this has the effects of [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239). In addition to [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) through the hummingbird, you can activate any [_aeon stone_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) within the _wayfinder_ as if the _wayfinder_ were still on your person. When the effect ends, the _wayfinder_ transforms back to normal in its current position. Damaging the bird ends the effect but does not destroy the _wayfinder_.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whisper of the First Lie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=471)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 617

**Price** 60,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This delicate necklace contains bottled whispers distilled from a source on the Astral Plane rumored to be connected to the first lie ever told. While wearing the necklace, you gain a +3 item bonus to Deception checks, and you can attempt to counteract effects that would force you to tell the truth or determine whether you are lying. Success on this counteract attempt lets you ignore the effect, rather than removing the effect entirely. The counteract level is 9, with a counteract modifier of +35.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact, envision, command;     **Effect** You unstopper the vial and release the lie, creating the effect of a [_fabricated truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=106) (DC 47). The vial is emptied and can never be activated again.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _fabricated truth_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>envision, interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The band of this ring is made from blue-colored iron and has two sharp, decorative protrusions on each side of the red-stone inlay. It feels quite heavy and reliable. Wearing this ring grants you a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks, but only if the associated checks involve haggling or bargaining over a purchase or trade.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Hunter's Arrowhead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=483)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #145: Hellknight Hill](/Sources.aspx?ID=4) pg. 77, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 153

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>command</row></row>

**Frequency** once per day

**Trigger** You would miss with an attack made with a bow.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This arrowhead-shaped charm is not meant to be affixed to an arrow, but instead to be carried in a pocket or inside of a quiver. A _hunter’s arrowhead_ is etched with images sacred to the elven god Ketephys. If you carry one on your person, you are infused with great skill at hunting, and gain a +1 item bonus to Survival skill checks and attack rolls against any creature you’ve currently designated as your prey for the [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=10) ranger ability (note that the bonus to attack rolls is an item bonus, and therefore not cumulative with an item bonus from a magic weapon). A _hunter's arrowhead_ is also a religious symbol of Ketephys.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hunter's Arrowhead

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bountiful Cauldron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=506)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 71

**Price** 680 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mid-sized, silver cauldron is a boon within areas where access to fresh food is limited, for it can be commanded to fill itself with hearty (and delicious) vegetable stew. It can also be put to a much greater use in the pursuit of crafting certain items. When used to Craft alchemical items, potions, or oils, a bountiful cauldron grants a +2 item bonus to the Crafting check.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You stir the cauldron, and it casts a 4th-level [_create food_](/Spells.aspx?ID=52) spell, filling itself with enough delicious vegetable stew to feed 12 Medium creatures.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloak of Repute](/Equipment.aspx?ID=510)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gaudy and extravagant cloak is adorned with brilliant gems and a fur trim. While wearing the cloak, you gain instant fame and notoriety, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to Make an Impression. Creatures attempting to learn about you gain the same bonus to Gather Information about you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wield your great stature to make a Request of a creature. If you roll a success on the check, you get a critical success instead.

</column>

item_bonus_note: Thievery checks to Pick a Lock
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Insistent Door Knocker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=511)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dark iron door knocker comes in a variety of shapes, such as a bird clutching a ring in its talons or a gargoyle holding a ring in its mouth. Placing the door knocker on the surface of a door (an Interact action) causes it to attach and remain in place. While in place, the door knocker whispers hints to you while you attempt to unlock the door, granting a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks to Pick a Lock. Removing the door knocker from a surface requires you to use an Interact action.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place the door knocker against a wall, floor, or ceiling. The door knocker fuses in place and creates a usable door within the surface. Any creature can move through the door normally, as if the door had always been there. The door can penetrate up to 1 foot of material, so thick material, such as heavy stone walls, can cause this effect to fail, expending its use for the day. A thin layer of metal in a wall, floor, or ceiling also causes the effect to fail. You can use an Interact action to release the door knocker from the surface and return the surface to its previous shape.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</column>

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the door knocker can penetrate up to 5 feet of material when activated.

level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Insistent Door Knocker (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and the door knocker can penetrate up to 10 feet of material when activated.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place the door knocker against a wall, floor, or ceiling. The door knocker fuses in place and creates a usable door within the surface. Any creature can move through the door normally, as if the door had always been there. The door can penetrate up to 1 foot of material, so thick material, such as heavy stone walls, can cause this effect to fail, expending its use for the day. A thin layer of metal in a wall, floor, or ceiling also causes the effect to fail. You can use an Interact action to release the door knocker from the surface and return the surface to its previous shape.

item_bonus_note: Deception checks to Impersonate an individual and to Lie while in character as that individual
markdown:
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** 1 --- This long, shoddy cloak bears a hood and a simple brass clasp. While wearing the cloak, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to Impersonate an individual and to Lie while in character as that individual. **Activate** 1 minute (envision, Interact); **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You are Impersonating someone else; **Effect** While conversing or otherwise casually interacting with other creatures, you can adjust the mantle’s clasp to modify those creatures’ recollections of the last 5 minutes of their interaction with you. Each creature must attempt a DC 25 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature recalls the interaction clearly and realizes you were trying to alter their memory.
**Success** The creature recalls the interaction clearly.
**Failure** You decide whether or not the creature recalls the interaction. You can also alter minor details the creature recalls about the interaction, such as the time, place, and whether the interaction was favorable, as determined by the GM.
**Critical Failure** You decide whether or not the creature recalls the interaction. You can also alter major details the creature recalls about the interaction, such as believing you to be another individual or that the creature agreed to a favor, as determined by the GM. Unmemorable Mantle **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Price** 300 gp **Bulk** 1 Unmemorable Mantle (Greater) **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Price** 1,300 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonus is +2, and the Will save DC is 28. Unmemorable Mantle (Major) **Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 73 **Price** 21,750 gp **Bulk** 1 --- The item bonus is +3, the Will save DC is 38, and you can activate the mantle once every 10 minutes.
name: Unmemorable Mantle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lastwall Soup](/Equipment.aspx?ID=516)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 92

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Knights of Lastwall has access to this option.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 minute (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crimson Reclaimers make this rich, hearty soup using herbs that baffle the senses of the undead. A bowl of Lastwall soup is as nourishing as a full meal. In addition, for 1 hour after consuming a bowl of Lastwall soup, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and DCs against undead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lastwall Soup

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sinister Knight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=518)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 92

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto heavy armor

</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Knights of Lastwall has access to this option.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Sinister knight_ armor shrouds the wearer’s identity in secrecy, allowing Crimson Reclaimers to pass among foes without being immediately unmasked. The wearer gains a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** With a thought, the wearer activates a disguise. While the _sinister knight_ armor’s disguise is active, any identifying insignia or aesthetic of the armor is replaced by generic malevolent aesthetics such as spikes or demonic faces. While in the disguise, the wearer is always considered to be taking precautions against [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), and even a creature that successfully notices the wearer with its lifesense mistakes the wearer for an undead unless it critically succeeds at its Perception check or the wearer critically fails a Deception or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check. Finally, while in the disguise, the rune attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=299) any effects that would reveal your alignment; on a successful counteract check, rather than negate the effect, the rune causes the effect to perceive your alignment as evil (maintaining any lawful or chaotic component of your alignment).

 The wearer can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=74) the disguise, and if the wearer’s armor is removed, the disguise deactivates automatically.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sinister Knight

item_bonus_note: Crafting checks related to stonemasonry
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Guiding Chisel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=541)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Age of Ashes</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Elf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=58" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #148: Fires of the Haunted City](/Sources.aspx?ID=13) pg. 76

**Price** 10,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical chisel evokes the holy symbol of the elven goddess of art and architecture, Findeladlara. The _Guiding Chisel’s_ original function was as a portal key to Duskgate, but as Alseta’s Ring faded from the collective memories of the elves, the chisel became known and used exclusively for its other abilities. Eventually, elven emissaries gave it to the dwarves of Saggorak as a gesture of friendship.

 You gain a +3 item bonus to Crafting checks related to stonemasonry. In addition, it takes you only 1 day to Craft items using the _Guiding Chisel_ instead of 4 days, and you quadruple the discount you get for spending additional days Crafting items with the _Guiding Chisel_. If it’s unclear whether you could use the _Guiding Chisel_ to Craft an item, the GM decides.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (command, envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You embed the _Guiding Chisel_ in the ground and imagine a building. After 10 minutes, you remove the chisel and the building appears. The building can take any shape you wish, filling up to ten contiguous 10-foot cubes within 1,000 feet of the chisel. The building lasts until you use the chisel to create another building. This building has the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Guiding Chisel

Granted by Katapesh’s Pactmasters to influential merchants, exceptional [Zephyr Guards](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=35), and favorite retainers, these blue crystal–studded platinum rings sharpen their wearers’ urban instincts. You gain a +3 item bonus to Mercantile [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks while wearing the ring, and you can attempt checks that require a proficiency rank of master in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14). While invested, the ring grants a +2 item bonus to saving throws while you are in an urban setting, and this increases to a +3 item bonus if you have legendary proficiency in Guild Lore, Katapesh Lore, Mercantile Lore, or Society.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glaive of the Artist](/Equipment.aspx?ID=567)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 122

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)
</row>

**Access** follower of  Shelyn 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) greater striking [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_ has a long, multi-tailed rainbow-colored banner affixed to the butt of the pole in the style of [Shelyn’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=17) religious symbol. While wielding the glaive, you gain a +2 item bonus on [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You furl one of the colored tails around the pole, causing the glaive to gain the effects of your choice of the [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [_shocking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), or [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) property runes for 1 round, depending on the tail you chose.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You thrust the blade of the glaive into the ground, activating the power of the banner. (The blade can penetrate any non-metallic surface when used in this way, and does not leave a mark when withdrawn.) As long as the glaive is thrust into the ground and standing upright, all allies within 60 feet who can see the glaive’s banner gain a +2 item bonus to Performance checks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glaive of the Artist

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You furl one of the colored tails around the pole, causing the glaive to gain the effects of your choice of the [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [_shocking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), or [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) property runes for 1 round, depending on the tail you chose.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You thrust the blade of the glaive into the ground, activating the power of the banner. (The blade can penetrate any non-metallic surface when used in this way, and does not leave a mark when withdrawn.) As long as the glaive is thrust into the ground and standing upright, all allies within 60 feet who can see the glaive’s banner gain a +2 item bonus to Performance checks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glaive of the Artist

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

**Bulk** L

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

This ornate headgear comes in many different shapes and sizes, though most are elaborately decorated with motifs of the deity whose honor in which it was made. The miter brings you spiritually closer to your deity’s servitors, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command);  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You chant for a minute about a task at hand for the coming week to cast a 4th-level [_read omens_](/Spells.aspx?ID=247) spell that grants you cryptic but useful advice.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Torag's Silver Anvil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=575)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gods & Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=17) pg. 123

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Meteor Hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=97)
</row>

**Access** follower of  Torag 

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This portable silver anvil has a narrow hoop on one end, through which a long, sturdy chain has been strung. _[Torag's](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) Silver Anvil_ can be wielded as a _+3 [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) [greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) [meteor hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=97)_. Outside of combat, the anvil can be used with a hammer as an incredibly effective portable forge, heating up the metal to be forged without a furnace and granting a +3 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks involving metalworking. When you use the anvil to successfully [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) a metal item, the item recovers an additional 10 Hit Points (or an additional 20 on a critical success).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Torag's Silver Anvil

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

</row>

</column>

---

A _bullhook_ is a stout rod about 4 feet long with a blunt hook on the end. Too dull to be useful as a weapon, a _bullhook_ is instead used to direct animals in training or in performing their tasks. A _bullhook_ grants you an item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) while you are holding it.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wave the _bullhook_ to produce the effects of a [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) spell. This spell loses the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) trait and can target only animals.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _command_ of the appropriate level.

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

This delicate and elegant diadem is woven of intricate silver wires set with three tear-shaped pearlescent moonstones. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> focus;  **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You are within 10 feet of a _moonstone pool_; **Effect** You peer into the _moonstone pool_ and gain access to all the visual history recorded within the receptacle by concentrating on the specific subject you wish to see. Conversely, you can choose to deposit your own memories into the pool by concentrating for 1 hour on the information you wish to impart.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Moonstone Diadem

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>

This _+1 resilient [studded leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)_ is adorned with colorful sequins that sparkle in even the faintest light. While wearing the spangled rider’s suit, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) you are riding. Any animal you ride automatically knows the [Drop Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=78) and [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=87) basic actions, in addition to any other basic actions it knows (normally only Leap, Seek, Stand, Stride, and Strike).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114))  **Trigger** You take damage while mounted or your mount Drops Prone;  **Effect** You dismount, moving off your mount into a space adjacent to it.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[Stole of Civility](/Equipment.aspx?ID=592)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Extinction Curse</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

Woven from swaths of rich brocade silk and embroidered with ancient Azlanti script in golden thread, this stole imparts a noble appearance to even the homeliest of individuals when draped over the shoulders. While wearing a stole of civility, you receive a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) with humans or [Make a Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of humans, and a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against humans. The stole also grants you full understanding of the [Azlanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=49) language.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** you are a human; **Effect** You gain 10 temporary Hit Points, which last for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stole of Civility

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dwarven War Axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=62)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The blade of this _+4 major striking [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=304) [dwarven waraxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=62)_ is carved with an intricate design depicting countless generations of great dwarven warriors and leaders. The axe has the [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon trait, in addition to the normal weapon traits for a dwarven waraxe. Strikes with the axe deal an additional 1d6 damage to orcs. While the axe is in your possession, you gain a +4 item bonus when [Crafting](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) armor, jewelry, metalworking, stonemasonry, snares, traps, and weapons. If you are a [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1), you gain [greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) while holding the axe. If you are not a dwarf, you are [stupefied 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) while holding the axe, and if you are an orc, you are also [drained 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and [enfeebled 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) while holding it.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** The axe casts a 10th-level [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320) spell to conjure an [elite elemental avalanche](/Monsters.aspx?ID=186). The spell is automatically sustained, requiring no action on your part but still allowing you to command the elemental on each of your turns. You can Dismiss the Spell.

**Destruction** If an orc beheads a dwarven king with the _Axe of the Dwarven Lords_, the axe rusts away to nothing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Axe of the Dwarven Lords

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 28"
    pfs=""
>
[Philosopher's Extractor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=617)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gamemastery Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=22) pg. 112

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 8
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bizarre machine is a complex arrangement of flasks, tubes, and other alchemical equipment. The _philosopher’s extractor_ is designed to create the ultimate alchemical concoctions. The extractor functions as an exceptional set of [alchemist’s tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2), granting a +4 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks related to alchemy. When using the extractor to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) an alchemical item or with infused reagents as part of your daily preparations, you can create impeccable alchemical items. An impeccable alchemical item always uses the maximum numerical value possible for any rolls it requires, such as dealing maximum damage with [alchemist’s fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75) or restoring the maximum number of Hit Points with an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91). If the impeccable item has a duration, it lasts twice as long as normal. Finally, an impeccable alchemical item never has a drawback.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You use the extractor to produce an alchemical item of your level or lower whose formula you know. The extractor can create 56 levels’ worth of items per day in this way. For example, the extractor could create two true elixirs of life (19th level) and two moderate elixirs of life (9th level), or eight [comprehension elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=88) (7th level), and so on.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 hour (Interact);     **Effect** You take a sizable portion of a creature (at least two-thirds of its original mass) and filter it through the mechanisms of the extractor. After the end of the process, the _philosopher’s extractor_ creates a transmogrifying [mutagen](/Traits.aspx?ID=116) that imparts the essence of the creature to the drinker. The extractor can make several transmogrifying mutagens simultaneously using the same activation if enough mass is provided at once, up to a maximum of 10 concurrent mutagens. Drinking a transmogrifying mutagen imparts you with one of the creature’s unique abilities for 1 hour. This could grant one of several abilities such as a dragon’s Breath Weapon, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), flight, [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17), or immunity to sleep. The ability functions as it did for the original creature, except it uses your class DC or your spell DC (whichever is higher) instead of the creature’s DC. The mutagen grants only abilities based on a creature’s physiology and never grants magic-related abilities such as innate spells or spellcasting ability. The GM ultimately decides what ability a transmogrifying mutagen grants.

Unlike normal for mutagens and [](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects, you can drink multiple transmogrifying mutagens and gain benefits from each. When you drink transmogrifying mutagens made from different creatures, you receive abilities from each creature. When you drink multiple transmogrifying mutagens made from the same type of creature, such as multiple mutagens made from trolls, you gain an additional ability for each transmogrifying mutagen you drink, and the duration of the abilities from that creature type increases by 1 hour for each additional concoction you drink.

 However, these additional benefits come with a risk; if you drink a transmogrifying mutagen while at least one other is active, after drinking it, you must succeed at a flat check with a DC equal to the total number of active transmogrifying mutagens you’ve consumed. On a failure, you fully transform into a member of the species of the latest transmogrifying mutagen you drank, and you almost always go berserk from your change. The transformation may leave some amount of your personality and memories intact, at the GM’s discretion.

**Destruction** Simultaneously feeding the philosopher’s extractor sizable portions of an aeon, angel, archon, azata, daemon, demon, devil, protean, and psychopomp, each of at least 14th level, along with a philosopher’s stone causes the device to jam, overheat, and explode. Creatures within 60 feet of the explosion must succeed at a DC 55 Reflex save or become covered in an alchemical slurry that transforms them into a horrifying amalgam of at least two of the creatures used to destroy the machine.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Philosopher's Extractor

This leather sheath is large enough to hold an item of up to light Bulk and is typically used for [daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [wands](/Equipment.aspx?Category=34), [thieves' tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=58), and similar objects. You can affix it to the inside of a boot, under a bracer or sleeve, or in other inconspicuous locations to gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and DCs to hide or conceal the item within.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Detective's Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=679)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 249

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This leather satchel contains empty vials, a pair of tweezers, a supply of small linen cloths, a set of brass calipers and a knotted string for measuring distances, several pieces of chalk, a pen, and a blank notebook for keeping notes. Every component of a detective's kit is of exceeding quality, and thus a detective's kit adds a +1 item bonus to checks to investigate a crime scene, a clue, or similar details. Like other tool kits, a detective's kit uses one hand if wearing the kit and two hands otherwise.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bloodhound Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=694)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 254

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wide, single-use mask is designed to be slipped over your mouth and nose and its alchemical filter activated all with one hand. Once activated, the mask sharpens odors, giving you [imprecise scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) with the listed range. You can't wear other masks while you're wearing a bloodhound mask. When you use [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a creature by its scent, your proficiency bonus is equal to your level even if you're untrained, and the mask grants you the listed item bonus to your Survival check. The GM sets the Survival DC based on the area's ability to hold scent rather than on visual clues, as normal for using scent.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skinstitch Salve](/Equipment.aspx?ID=698)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 255

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sticky salve stubbornly holds wounds closed and encourages swift natural healing. You can activate the salve in either of the following ways.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) without requiring [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25). You either gain a +2 item bonus to the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check, or you can use the skinstitch salve's Medicine modifier of +13 instead of your own.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> Interact;   **Trigger** You [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57) or use [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=760);  **Effect** You gain a +2 item bonus to the triggering [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check. If you roll a success on the Medicine check, you get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skinstitch Salve

A gecko potion is a sticky, tawny brown liquid with flecks of sand suspended in it. For 5 minutes after drinking this potion, your fingertips sprout thousands of microscopic, bristled hairs that cling to objects, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) and [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=67), and to your Reflex DC against [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) attempts.

For 5 minutes after drinking this potion, your fingertips sprout thousands of microscopic, bristled hairs that cling to objects, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) and [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=67), and to your Reflex DC against [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) attempts.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Glamorous Buckler](/Equipment.aspx?ID=734)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _glamorous buckler_ (Hardness 3, HP 6, BT 3) is lavishly decorated with gilding and inset gemstones that glitter in the light. While you have it raised, the _glamorous buckler_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You Feint; **Requirements** You have the _glamorous buckler_ raised; **Effect** As you Feint, the _glamorous buckler_ sparkles mightily. On a successful Feint, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Lover's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=737)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 261

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These white silk gloves are adorned in red hearts that glow faintly whenever you are adjacent to someone you feel particularly strongly toward. They buoy your spirit, giving you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grasp the hands of a willing creature you have strong positive feelings about, regardless of the nature of those feelings. The creature gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws and 10 temporary Hit Points for 10 minutes. If the creature shares your feelings, you gain the same benefits, and for the duration, when you both roll a success on a saving throw against an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect that causes negative emotions, you both get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lover's Gloves

    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mask of the Banshee](/Equipment.aspx?ID=738)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 261

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ice-blue half-mask is adorned with a wicked silver grin that covers the wearer's mouth, leaving the rest of the face uncovered. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The mask emits a soul-chilling scream that deals 6d10 negative damage to each living creature in a 20-foot emanation (DC 25 basic Fortitude save).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_wail of the banshee_](/Spells.aspx?ID=361).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

</traits>

pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Intimidation is +3. Activating a _greater mask of the banshee_ casts a 9th-level [_wail of the banshee_](/Spells.aspx?ID=361) (DC 41) affecting all living creatures in the area.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vestige Lenses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=767)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 25

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These simple lenses are alchemically treated to detect the faint smoke trail from a piece of specially formulated sandalwood incense. This incense has an odor undetectable by most people and a long burn time, making it perfect for discreetly tracking individuals. A stick of this incense costs 1 sp and can safely burn for up to 8 hours. While wearing the lenses, you see alchemical fumes in a distinct green tint, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a creature marked with the incense and to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) any alchemical vapors. If the smoke is fresh, Tracking via the incense's fumes might use a lower DC than normal for tracking a creature walking on firm surfaces.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vestige Lenses

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gummy liquid disproportionately lengthens your legs, causing you to grow but making your movement awkward. The effect is very noticeable unless your clothes can adequately conceal it.

 **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and you gain the listed status bonus to your Speed. In addition, you grow one size larger, but your larger size does not have any additional effects beyond the potential change in space.

 **Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to AC and a –2 penalty to Reflex saves.

This gummy liquid disproportionately lengthens your legs, causing you to grow but making your movement awkward. The effect is very noticeable unless your clothes can adequately conceal it.

 **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and you gain the listed status bonus to your Speed. In addition, you grow one size larger, but your larger size does not have any additional effects beyond the potential change in space.

 **Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to AC and a –2 penalty to Reflex saves.

This [caligni](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=18)-made, murky-green drug supercharges a creature's olfactory senses at the expense of dulling their mind. Popular throughout the Darklands for its ability to transform normally dull scents and flavors into intense, euphoric experiences, grolna can also be a great aid to trackers who want to follow scent trails, turning ordinary humanoids into effective bloodhounds.

 Taking an additional dose of grolna while affected by the effects of stage 1 of a previous dose resets the duration of stage 1 and postpones the onset of stage 2 effects.

 **Saving Throw** DC 13 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 8 hours; **Stage 1** gain [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet, gain a +3 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to follow tracks, and become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 hour); **Stage 2** stupefied 2 (6 hours)

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Agents of Edgewatch</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L

---

A patchwork of humanoid flesh makes up this grotesque mask stitched together with black silk or wire. It is distinctive for its bulbous orange eye— crafted from a magical glass bauble—and wide row of teeth. When worn, the mask amplifies your ability to sense fear in other creatures. You automatically know the value of the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition of any [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27) creature, and you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) frightened creatures. Whenever you deal precision damage to a frightened creature, you deal 1 additional precision damage. If you are not evil, you are [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) while wearing the _Skinsaw mask_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Writ of Authenticity](/Equipment.aspx?ID=842)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 26

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Players can gain access to faction-specific gear by taking the corresponding Faction Gear Access Game Reward, available when they reach 20 reputation with the respective faction._

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** Membership in the Envoy's Alliance Pathfinder Society faction.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A typical Pathfinder writ of authenticity confirms the holder's status as a member of the Pathfinder Society and their intended business. Most writs are pieces of fine parchment folded, bound with gold cord, and sealed with three wax seals. The top seal is always the Glyph of the Open Road and the middle one is the Envoy's Alliance open door emblem. The bottom seal uses the preparer's personal sigil. Inside, the text describes a specific instance of a legitimate business activity, with blanks for the Pathfinder to fill in and personalize the writ to the persons and activity desired. Presenting a writ of authenticity grants a +2 item bonus to [Make a Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) for the associated business, assuming the writ is legitimate and the viewer expects to work with the Pathfinder Society for that activity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Astrolabes can be used for navigation in unfamiliar or featureless locations. To use an astrolabe, the holder must be trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16). By spending 1 minute to measure the height of the stars and planets, a holder who knows the time and date can determine the latitude, and a holder who knows their latitude can determine the date and time. An astrolabe also grants a +1 item bonus on checks to identify celestial bodies. A standard astrolabe only functions on steady ground.

A standard deck of cards consists of 54 cards made from thick paper contained within a paper sleeve. The most common deck used for games and gambling is known as the Old Mage deck and features four suits themed with the four essences of magic, each with 13 cards, as well as two wildcards. The name and appearance of the deck varies from region to region, such as the Magician's Deck in Taldor or the Deck of Masks in the Shackles.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

A deck of marked playing cards appears to be an ordinary deck upon initial inspection but bears specific folds, colors, scratches, or other markings on each card that allow an informed user to know what's on the other side of a card. A deck of marked cards grants you a +1 item bonus to [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) checks or other relevant checks to gamble with the cards. You can determine that an unattended deck of cards is marked with a successful DC 20 Perception check. Determining that a deck is marked during play is more difficult and is typically determined by a Perception check against the dealer's Games Lore or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) DC.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rubbing Set](/Equipment.aspx?ID=874)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 115

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

By using a rubbing set, you can quickly make a copy of carvings or etchings you find during your explorations, making them easier to accurately reproduce. This kit consists of a roll of thin onion-skin paper in a waterproof leather case, several thick wax crayons, and a bar of sealing wax that can be melted and used to temporarily hold the paper in place while you take the rubbing. Using the kit consumes the materials and provides a +1 item bonus on checks to document or reproduce artwork or writing that has been carved, etched, or otherwise physically added to a surface.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rubbing Set

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spectacles of Understanding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=882)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 117

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to this item_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Member of the Pathfinder Society.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These folding, wood-framed spectacles hold flat discs of cut crystal as lenses and are held in place with thin cords that wrap around each ear, with another strap holding them around the neck while not in use. While you are wearing the spectacles, you gain a +1 item bonus to checks to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Unfolding the spectacles onto the bridge of the nose, you gain the effects of 2nd-level [_comprehend language_](/Spells.aspx?ID=46) for one hour except that it applies to all languages you see rather than a single language, and it does not apply to language you hear.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spectacles of Understanding
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 117

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spectacles of Understanding (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 117

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and the ability to _comprehend writing_ from comprehend language lasts until you fold up the spectacles or the item is no longer invested by you, whichever comes first, rather than lasting 1 hour.
name: Spectacles of Understanding

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Sun Wheel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=905)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Troubles in Otari</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Troubles in Otari](/Sources.aspx?ID=55) pg. 60

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Created by the priests of a Qadiran sect of [Sarenites](/Deities.aspx?ID=16), the _sun wheels_ were designed to protect the Dawnflower's followers in their quest to hunt down a dangerous cult trying to raise a spawn of the evil god [Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=15). Once they completed their crusade, these priests disbanded and went on to minister other congregations. They each took their _sun wheels_ with them, handing them down to the next generations.

 This copper medallion is etched with a stylized sun, its rays shaped like the spokes of a wagon wheel. It tends to glint brightly when exposed to sunlight. While wearing this pendant, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks. You must be good-aligned to use the sun wheel's true power: the following activation, which provides you a shield of burning force.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> concentrate;     **Effect** The _sun wheel_ casts a [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) spell benefiting you. When you use the spell to prevent damage, you prevent 10 damage instead of 5. If the attacker is adjacent to you, you can choose to deal 2d6 fire damage to it, which it gets a DC 21 basic Reflex save to resist.

 If you choose to deal the fire damage, you can't Activate _the sun wheel_ again until the next morning. If you prevent damage but don't deal the fire damage, you can use it again in 10 minutes, as normal for _shield_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Wheel

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs=""
>
[Secret-Keeper's Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=914)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each of these full-face masks is made of stitched materials—often including humanoid skin—and has a single, bulbous eye. While wearing the mask, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) and the mask doesn't impair your vision. The mask can also be activated, with an ability based on its type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs=""
>
Secret-Keeper's Mask (Blackfingers)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This featureless mask is made of thin, papery skin. Its single eye is a fragile bubble of venom-filled glass.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twist the mask's eye and peer at an object. You know whether the object is poison or has been poisoned, but you don't learn the number or type of poisons present in the object.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs=""
>
Secret-Keeper's Mask (Gray Master)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This monochrome mask is made of tattered rags of gray and black cloth and bleached fragments of skin.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You succeed on a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) an item of negligible Bulk;  **Effect** You create an illusory duplicate of the item you just stole. This duplicate has the appropriate appearance and weight, but it doesn't make sounds or generate smells. The illusion vanishes after 10 minutes, or earlier if anyone examines or manipulates the illusory duplicate (such as to try to use it).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs=""
>
Secret-Keeper's Mask (Father Skinsaw)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This mask is a patchwork of tanned skins, each with a distinctive scar. It is contorted into a caricature of a face with a leering eye.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You deal [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to a creature;  **Effect** The bleeding wound you cause is particularly mutilating. The DC of flat checks to recover from the persistent bleed damage is increased by 2, and the target creature has a –2 status penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks while it is bleeding and for 1 minute after it ceases bleeding.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs=""
>
Secret-Keeper's Mask (Reaper of Reputation)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This mask is made of gray and black tiles sewn together. It has a messy, inky spiral over the mouth.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact (auditory, linguistic);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch the mask's spiral and identify a target creature within 30 feet. For the next 10 minutes, any normal speech you utter can, at your discretion, be inaudible to all creatures except the target creature, who can hear you clearly regardless of ambient sound so long as it is within 1 mile. This ability doesn't silence magical words, such as the verbal component of spellcasting or commands to activate magic items.
name: Secret-Keeper's Mask (Blackfingers)

<traits>

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each of these full-face masks is made of stitched materials—often including humanoid skin—and has a single, bulbous eye. While wearing the mask, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) and the mask doesn't impair your vision. The mask can also be activated, with an ability based on its type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs=""
>
Secret-Keeper's Mask (Blackfingers)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This featureless mask is made of thin, papery skin. Its single eye is a fragile bubble of venom-filled glass.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twist the mask's eye and peer at an object. You know whether the object is poison or has been poisoned, but you don't learn the number or type of poisons present in the object.

level="2"

pfs=""

Secret-Keeper's Mask (Gray Master)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM's Toolkit: Secret Keeper's Mask](/Sources.aspx?ID=67)

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This monochrome mask is made of tattered rags of gray and black cloth and bleached fragments of skin.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You succeed on a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) an item of negligible Bulk;  **Effect** You create an illusory duplicate of the item you just stole. This duplicate has the appropriate appearance and weight, but it doesn't make sounds or generate smells. The illusion vanishes after 10 minutes, or earlier if anyone examines or manipulates the illusory duplicate (such as to try to use it).

This mask is a patchwork of tanned skins, each with a distinctive scar. It is contorted into a caricature of a face with a leering eye.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You deal [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to a creature;  **Effect** The bleeding wound you cause is particularly mutilating. The DC of flat checks to recover from the persistent bleed damage is increased by 2, and the target creature has a –2 status penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks while it is bleeding and for 1 minute after it ceases bleeding.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cape of the Open Sky](/Equipment.aspx?ID=948)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 74

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This cloth of gold cape was originally gifted to one of Goka's emperors centuries ago. Since then, it has spawned many imitations, which are all alike in that they are dyed with an elaborate seal depicting two drakes flanking Goka's palace and the Seven Dragons Bridge. The beautiful cape grants you a +3 item bonus to checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) and to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50). While you wear the cape, the weather (including wind and fog) doesn't affect your movement or vision. Additionally, whenever you fall while wearing the _cape of the open sky_, the cape automatically casts [_feather fall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=111) on you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** The cape becomes a pair of golden drake wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet for 1 hour.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The cloak pushes you onward. You Stride or [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to your Speed, but you can move only in a straight line. You gain a +15-foot status bonus to your Speed and fly Speed for this movement.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cape of the Open Sky

item_bonus_note: Astronomy Lore
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Malevolence **Source** [Malevolence](/Sources.aspx?ID=88) pg. 67 **Price** 650 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Even during the time of Thassilon, when the Order of the Starless Night still existed, _starless scopes_ were uncommon. Today, this final scope might well be the last one to survive the passage of time. A _starless scope_ is a handheld telescope crafted of silver and fine wood. When one peers at the sky through a _starless scope_, one always views the sky as if during a clear night. A starless scope adds a +2 item bonus to [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and Perception checks made to observe the skies, as it causes all celestial objects to dim when not directly observed, allowing its user to more easily observe their intended subject. It also grants a +2 item bonus to all [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24). A starless scope also functions as a divine focus for worshipers of [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5). The magic of a starless scope requires a specially prepared lens to function, and it's possible to shatter the lens if you roll poorly when activating the _starless scope_. Without a functional lens, a _starless scope_ doesn't function. You can replace a shattered lens with a functional lens by taking a two-action activity with the manipulate trait. If you know the formula for the starless scope, you can [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) a new lens with a DC 25 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) (master) check. A starless scope lens is a level 9 item worth 100 gp. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** you use the _starless scope_ as a divine focus; **Requirements** you worship Desna; **Effect** You attempt to enhance the effects of a spell as you cast it by attempting a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check against the spell's DC, as determined by [Table 10–5](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) **Critical Success** Any attack rolls you make with the spell gain a +2 status bonus, and any saving throw a foe makes against the spell takes a –2 status penalty. If you would normally cast the spell from a spell slot, and the spell is 5th level or lower, you don't spend the spell slot and cast the spell from the _starless scope_ instead.
**Success** Any attack rolls you make with the spell gain a +1 status bonus, and any saving throw a foe makes against the spell takes a –1 status penalty.
**Failure** The spell is cast normally. Attempt a DC 11 flat check. On a failure, the _starless scope's_ lens shatters.

If it's [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it's concealed rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The creature takes a –1 status penalty to saving throws against [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) effects while it shimmers with starlight.
**Failure** As success, but the creature also suffers an additional effect. An [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creature loses its incorporeal trait as long as it shines with starlight. Any other creature becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) as long as it shines with starlight.

item_bonus_note: Occultism checks made to Identify Magic
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Malevolence **Source** [Malevolence](/Sources.aspx?ID=88) pg. 67 **Price** 650 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- Even during the time of Thassilon, when the Order of the Starless Night still existed, _starless scopes_ were uncommon. Today, this final scope might well be the last one to survive the passage of time. A _starless scope_ is a handheld telescope crafted of silver and fine wood. When one peers at the sky through a _starless scope_, one always views the sky as if during a clear night. A starless scope adds a +2 item bonus to [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and Perception checks made to observe the skies, as it causes all celestial objects to dim when not directly observed, allowing its user to more easily observe their intended subject. It also grants a +2 item bonus to all [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) checks made to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24). A starless scope also functions as a divine focus for worshipers of [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5). The magic of a starless scope requires a specially prepared lens to function, and it's possible to shatter the lens if you roll poorly when activating the _starless scope_. Without a functional lens, a _starless scope_ doesn't function. You can replace a shattered lens with a functional lens by taking a two-action activity with the manipulate trait. If you know the formula for the starless scope, you can [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) a new lens with a DC 25 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) (master) check. A starless scope lens is a level 9 item worth 100 gp. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** you use the _starless scope_ as a divine focus; **Requirements** you worship Desna; **Effect** You attempt to enhance the effects of a spell as you cast it by attempting a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check against the spell's DC, as determined by [Table 10–5](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) **Critical Success** Any attack rolls you make with the spell gain a +2 status bonus, and any saving throw a foe makes against the spell takes a –2 status penalty. If you would normally cast the spell from a spell slot, and the spell is 5th level or lower, you don't spend the spell slot and cast the spell from the _starless scope_ instead.
**Success** Any attack rolls you make with the spell gain a +1 status bonus, and any saving throw a foe makes against the spell takes a –1 status penalty.
**Failure** The spell is cast normally. Attempt a DC 11 flat check. On a failure, the _starless scope's_ lens shatters.
**Critical Failure** The spell is disrupted and the _starless scope's_ lens shatters. **Activate** Interact ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You raise the scope to your eye and observe a creature within 30 feet. You can attempt to identify the creature by [Recalling Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26). The _starless scope_ adds a +2 item bonus to this check. If the creature is associated with the Dominion of the Black or the Elder Mythos, or is a creature whose actions have blasphemed against Desna (Ioseff Xarwin's ghost falls into this latter category), the creature must attempt a DC 27 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, and the _starless scope's_ lens shatters.
**Success** The creature shines with starlight for 1 round. During this time, the creature can't be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) if it is visible. If it's [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26), it's concealed rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39). The creature takes a –1 status penalty to saving throws against [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) effects while it shimmers with starlight.
**Failure** As success, but the creature also suffers an additional effect. An [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creature loses its incorporeal trait as long as it shines with starlight. Any other creature becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) as long as it shines with starlight.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration of the starlight is 1 minute, and the status penalty to saving throws against divine effects increases to –2.
